title: Quantum metric nonlinear Hall effect in a topological antiferromagnetic heterostructure
classification_notes: GPT error: Unterminated string starting at: line 181 column 35 (char 11300)

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports an intrinsic second-order nonlinear Hall effect in a PT-symmetric antiferromagnetic heterostructure made from even-layer MnBi2Te4 interfaced with black phosphorus. The effect is attributed to a quantum metric dipole of the Fermi-surface states, is switchable by reversing the antiferromagnetic order, and is used to demonstrate wireless microwave rectification without external bias or magnetic field.
measurement_types: nonlinear electrical transport / second-harmonic Hall voltage (V2ω)
gate-dependent transport (top gate, bottom gate, vertical electric field)
magnetic-field-dependent transport (Bz sweeps to prepare AFM states)
microwave / RF rectification (DC output under RF excitation)

figures.description: Conceptual comparison of nonlinear transport in a PN junction as a charge-induced nonlinear device.
Conceptual comparison of nonlinear transport in noncentrosymmetric conductors.
Conceptual illustration of spin-induced electrical nonlinearity in PT-symmetric antiferromagnets.
Lattice structures of MnBi2Te4 and black phosphorus and their heterostructure geometry.
Angle-resolved four-probe resistance of 6SL MnBi2Te4 before and after BP interfacing, showing isotropy then 180° anisotropy.
Optical SHG response showing symmetry breaking after BP interfacing.
Nonlinear Hall voltage V2ω_yxx before and after BP interfacing, showing emergence of a nonlinear Hall signal only after BP is added.
Device schematic of 2L BP / 6SL MnBi2Te4 / 2L BP with aligned crystalline axes.
Longitudinal and Hall components of the nonlinear voltage, showing Hall-dominant response.
Magnetic-field sweep protocols used to prepare the two antiferromagnetic states under fixed vertical electric field.
Linear longitudinal voltage versus current for AFM-I and AFM-II, showing unchanged Ohmic response.
Nonlinear Hall voltage versus current for AFM-I and AFM-II, showing sign reversal with AFM order.
Temperature dependence of the nonlinear Hall signal, present in the AFM phase and absent in the nonmagnetic phase.
Scaling of nonlinear Hall conductivity σ2ω_yxx with Drude conductivity σxx, used to argue dissipationless behavior.
Angular dependence of nonlinear Hall conductivity versus current direction.
Dual-gate resistance map versus top-gate and bottom-gate voltages, showing independent tuning of carrier density and vertical electric field.
Dependence of nonlinear Hall conductivity and linear resistance on vertical electric field Ez.
Schematic comparison of Berry curvature dipole and quantum metric dipole under AFM-I and AFM-II, used to exclude Berry-curvature-dipole origin.
Measured nonlinear Hall conductivity versus carrier density.
Theoretically calculated nonlinear Hall conductivity versus carrier density from the heterostructure model.
Calculated Fermi surface and quantum metric components for the decoupled system with C3z symmetry.
Integral kernel (v_y g_xx - v_x g_yx) around the Fermi surface, showing cancellation with C3z symmetry and nonzero result after symmetry breaking.
Calculated band structure of the BP/6SL MnBi2Te4/BP heterostructure with quantum metric color scale.
Measured microwave rectification DC voltage under RF excitation, including frequency dependence.
Schematic illustrations of microwave rectification and quantum-metric-induced nonlinear responses.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements including angle-resolved resistance, linear and second-harmonic transport voltages (Vω, V2ω), temperature sweeps, magnetic-field sweeps to prepare AFM states, gate-dependent transport maps, optical SHG measurements, polarized Raman measurements, and microwave/RF rectification measurements. The text does not specify raw file formats or repository paths.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, or downloadable dataset description is present in the provided text. The paper does mention supplementary figures/tables and supplementary methods, but not a public data archive in the excerpt.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as experimental measurements collected on fabricated BP/MnBi2Te4 heterostructure devices using lock-in transport, SHG, Raman, and RF rectification setups. The text does not specify file formats, instrument output files, or a repository.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted nonlinear Hall conductivity σ2ω_yxx, Drude conductivity σxx, resistance maps, angular dependence curves, temperature dependence curves, and carrier-density-dependent conductivity curves. The paper also references summary table S1 for 26 devices and supplementary figures for additional characterization and mechanism checks.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied for the main figures and supplementary figures (S1–S28). The paper states that all 26 devices are summarized in fig. S15 and table S1, and that additional data supporting mechanism exclusion and modeling are in the supplementary figures. However, no explicit statement about source-data files is included in the excerpt.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned in the provided text. Theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations are described, but implementation scripts are not provided.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental-theoretical package. Experimental data are device-level transport and optical measurements; theoretical data are calculated band structures, quantum metric distributions, and model-derived nonlinear conductivities. The paper emphasizes that nonlinear Hall data are often obtained by taking the difference between two AFM domains. Supplementary materials contain methods, symmetry analysis, alternative-mechanism checks, additional data, and tables summarizing devices and parameters.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct measurements of resistance, nonlinear voltages, SHG, Raman, and microwave rectification on fabricated devices, indicating raw experimental data were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents extracted quantities such as σ2ω_yxx, σxx, resistance maps, angular dependence, and device summaries in figures, tables, and supplementary figures, indicating processed/source-data-style outputs.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Relevant phrases include: 'see fig. S15 and table S1 for a summary of all 26 devices', 'Supplementary Materials ... Figs. S1 to S28 Tables S1 to S3', and the methods sections describing measurement and modeling procedures.
